description: Schema for the information-book semantic-triple graph. Node types and predicates are the contract that the parser emits and the viewer consumes. Slugs are stable across outline renumbering; chapter ids look like 'chapter:transport-vs-selection'.

Part.description: One of the four Parts of the book

Chapter.description: A chapter in the outline (incl. 5b, 5c sub-chapters)

Note.description: A markdown file in information-book/ (prose or conversation subtype)

Mechanism.description: A named structural mechanism the book argues for (Transport, Selection, Want-as-prime-mover, ...)

PipelineStage.description: A node in the abstraction pipeline (Raw Data, Theory, News, Meme, ...)

Gate.description: A selection-side gate paired with a PipelineStage

Concept.description: Cross-cutting concept (receiver budget, complexity ceiling, manipulation surface, ...)

Question.description: A foundational open question the book is working out

Claim.description: A load-bearing claim the book argues — emerges from prose via harvesting + author promotion

Source.description: Book / paper / post / personal conversation the book engages with

Author.description: Author of one or more Sources

Tradition.description: Intellectual lineage (memetics, media ecology, network epistemology, ...)

CaseStudy.description: Worked example the book uses (Hammer/Copernicus, telephone game, matplotlib/seaborn, astrology, ...)

Tension.description: An open contradiction or unresolved trade-off

Status.description: Drafting status (drafted / in-workshop / skeleton / not-yet-drafted / superseded)

covers.description: Chapter discusses this Concept / Mechanism / Question / Claim / Tension

definedIn.description: Concept / Mechanism has its primary development in this Note

succeedsStage.description: PipelineStage comes after the previous stage

gateFor.description: Gate is the selection-side sibling of this PipelineStage

representedBy.description: Source is materially represented as this Note (e.g. conversation transcript that doubles as a source)

precondition.description: X is a precondition for Y

selectsFor.description: Gate selects for this Concept (its filter criterion)

evidencedBy.description: Claim / Concept / Mechanism evidenced by a Note or Source passage

supersedes.description: Replaces a prior version (Claim → Claim, Chapter → Chapter)

flagsOpenQuestion.description: Flags an open Question / unresolved issue

argues.description: Chapter or Note argues this Claim

supports.description: Source supports this Claim / Mechanism / Concept (with quote + confidence + rationale)

pressureTests.description: Source pressure-tests / challenges this Claim / Mechanism / Concept

dependsOn.description: Claim depends on another Claim, or on a Question resolving a particular way

mentions.description: Free-text mention — useful for navigation, not for argument

claim.description: Argument structure: arguing, supporting, pressure-testing, depending

weak.description: Free-text mentions — provenance not yet validated

noteSubtypes.prose: Standard chapter or thematic essay (H2-section structure)
noteSubtypes.conversation: Transcribed conversation (turn-based structure with speaker tags)
